sourceEvidence.label: Noctalia AI call, quota, job, and persistence boundaries

assessment.summary: Existing quota claims and image jobs support one higher-leverage opportunity: centralize paid AI authorization and lifecycle state behind a durable server command.

nonNegotiables: Preserve local dream capture and offline mutations.
Keep native speech recognition as the primary path and server transcription as Android fallback only.
Do not deploy or mutate production without explicit authorization.
assumptions: No measured production latency, cost, queue-depth, or abuse-volume budget was supplied.
Existing user-owned voice-path edits are unrelated source drift and remain untouched.

opportunities.title: Centralize paid AI command ownership
opportunities.summary: Make one durable server command own actor verification, cost controls, quota, idempotency, state transitions, and worker dispatch.
opportunities.diagnosis: Paid-work controls exist but end at different client and server boundaries, causing redundant calls, ambiguous retries, and state races.

evidence.claim: Quick categorization is awaited before durable dream creation.
The client owns quota preflight, pending persistence, analysis, image enqueue, result merge, and final persistence.
The image worker invokes a prompt model when the client submits the transcript despite analysis already returning imagePrompt.
A durable actor-bound idempotent queue exists for generate_image jobs.
Automatic retries on paid routes without durable result idempotency can repeat provider work after a lost response.

desiredInvariants: A valid dream is durable before optional AI enrichment begins.
Every paid provider invocation is authorized server-side against a verified actor immediately before work.
Every paid command has one stable idempotency key and one durable lifecycle record.
Actor burst, concurrency, global backlog, input, and output budgets are enforced before provider work.
Analysis persistence and initial image enqueue reuse one structured analysis result and image prompt.

options.title: Strengthen local controls
options.summary: Keep client orchestration while adding save-first behavior, strict bounds, idempotency, quota snapshots, targeted reads, and polling backoff.

evidenceCoverage.rationale: Saving before enrichment directly removes AI from the capture critical path.
Round trips shrink, but the client still owns the analysis lifecycle.

tradeoffs.assessment: Known inputs, retries, and bursts gain explicit controls, but future routes can still drift.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Exercise every paid route with malformed, oversized, duplicate, burst, and concurrent requests.

tradeoffs.assessment: Save latency, polling calls, and completed-image bytes should decrease; synchronous analysis remains.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Compare capture latency, polling count, and bytes per image completion on the same fixture journal.

tradeoffs.assessment: No new durable worker or large cache is introduced.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Compare client heap during capture and image reconciliation.

tradeoffs.assessment: Capture no longer depends on AI, but a lost synchronous analysis response remains ambiguous.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Inject provider timeouts and lost responses before and after provider completion.

tradeoffs.assessment: Existing deployments remain, with additional rejection metrics.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Confirm dashboards can segment bounded-request rejections without content logging.

tradeoffs.assessment: Changes are incremental and become tactical protections for later options.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Run supported-client compatibility and focused regression tests.

residualRisks: Client-owned analysis persistence and long-request ambiguity remain.

workPackages: Add shared bounds and save-first behavior.
Add targeted reconciliation and polling backoff.
acceptanceCriteria: Completed image refresh reads one dream.
implementationReadiness.rollback: Revert focused client and route changes.

options.title: Server-owned durable AI command
options.summary: Extend ai_jobs and claim-before-cost transactions so the server owns analysis authorization, state, persistence, and image dispatch.

evidenceCoverage.rationale: Capture is committed before the command.
Server state replaces client lifecycle authority for migrated analysis.
The analysis worker passes imagePrompt directly to the image job.
The existing job state machine is extended, while worker claim tests remain required.
Duplicate commands return the same durable lifecycle row; legacy routes still require conservative retries during migration.

tradeoffs.assessment: One server boundary owns actor, quota, idempotency, concurrency, burst, and backlog checks.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Run duplicate and concurrent transaction tests and prove provider mocks remain untouched on rejection.

tradeoffs.assessment: Acknowledgement becomes short and one prompt call disappears, while completion gains a queue hop.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Measure acknowledgement, queue age, completion, and provider calls per dream against the synchronous baseline.

tradeoffs.assessment: Bounded job rows and worker payloads add storage but no unbounded client cache.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Measure row growth and worker memory at configured concurrency.

tradeoffs.assessment: Lost responses and restarts reconcile from durable state; stuck jobs become an explicit operational failure mode.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Restart client and worker in every job state and verify deterministic reconciliation.

tradeoffs.assessment: Analysis queue age, retries, and stuck jobs require dashboards and recovery tooling.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Exercise alerts and a manual re-drive in a non-production environment.

tradeoffs.assessment: Schema, worker, route, and client protocol must coexist during rollout.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Use an additive migration and client feature flag, then test old and new builds against the same backend.

residualRisks: Compromised accounts and distributed guest installations can still consume legitimate-looking capacity.
Global provider budget exhaustion requires a separate circuit breaker.

workPackages: Add atomic command and job type.
Migrate authenticated client behind a flag.
Add Realtime after polling recovery is proven.
acceptanceCriteria: One idempotent command owns each analysis.
Server persists analysis and enqueues imagePrompt.
Cost controls reject before provider work.
migrationNotes: Migrate authenticated dreams first; keep synchronous guest compatibility.
implementationReadiness.rollback: Disable the client flag and command route, then drain accepted jobs.

options.title: Split functions and push completion
options.summary: Add the durable command while separating API failure domains and using private Realtime as the normal completion signal.

evidenceCoverage.rationale: Server jobs own lifecycle and private events only notify clients.
Workers and command APIs are isolated by authority and saturation domain.

tradeoffs.assessment: Scoped functions reduce credential and saturation blast radius if deployment permissions are also scoped.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Inventory environment authority and simulate Gemini saturation while testing quota and session endpoints.

tradeoffs.assessment: Isolation can reduce contention but adds independent cold starts and routing paths.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Load-test current and split deployments using production-like concurrency.

tradeoffs.assessment: More function instances and channel state increase aggregate runtime memory.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Compare aggregate billed runtime and peak memory by function.

tradeoffs.assessment: AI saturation is contained, while more distributed dependencies introduce configuration failures.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Run fault injection per function and verify fallback status recovery when Realtime is unavailable.

tradeoffs.assessment: Deployments, secrets, dashboards, and rollback coordination multiply.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Complete a staged deploy and rollback rehearsal before adoption.

tradeoffs.assessment: Stable routing contracts and multi-function rollout are required.
tradeoffs.validationPlan: Run old and new clients through compatibility routing during a canary window.

residualRisks: Realtime delivery can be lost and must not replace durable status.
Configuration drift across functions can recreate control inconsistencies.

workPackages: Split one boundary at a time.
Add private Broadcast with polling recovery.
acceptanceCriteria: AI saturation does not degrade guest or quota APIs.
Cross-user events are impossible under private-channel tests.
migrationNotes: Defer until source-derived risk is supported by runtime evidence.
implementationReadiness.rollback: Restore stable routes to the monolithic API while retaining durable jobs.

opportunities.recommendation: Implement the durable command incrementally, retaining local controls and deferring function splitting until production telemetry justifies its operational cost.

openQuestions: What measured Plus workload should set actor burst and concurrency defaults?
Should automatic categorization remain as a non-blocking job or be removed until explicit analysis?
Is production guest AI intentionally Android-only?
What global backlog threshold matches the Supabase and provider budgets?
